statement.markdown: Implement an in-place bubble sort algorithm. The function should sort the input array from smallest to largest. If the input array is already sorted, the function should return true. Otherwise, it should return false.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the input array is sorted or not
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n^2), Space complexity: O(1)

examples.explanation: The input array is already sorted in ascending order.
The input array is not sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Check if the function is correctly implementing the bubble sort algorithm

editorial.title: Bubble Sort
editorial.intuition: Bubble sort works by repeatedly swapping the adjacent elements if they are in wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: The function should iterate through the array until no more swaps are needed or the array is sorted.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the in-place bubble sort algorithm with a flag to check if any swaps were made.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function is correct because it correctly implements the bubble sort algorithm and checks if any swaps were made.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run will show that the function correctly sorts the array and returns true if the array is already sorted.
commonMistakes: Not implementing the bubble sort algorithm correctly
